Sec. 1  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is a major city in Nigeria, known for its economic growth and cultural significance. It is one of the most developed cities in the country and serves as a hub for business and education. The city is also home to several important institutions, including the University of Lagos and the Lagos State Government.  

Sec. 2  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is located in the Lagos State and is one of the fastest-growing cities in Nigeria. It has a diverse population and is known for its modern infrastructure and vibrant economy. The city is also a center for various industries, including manufacturing, retail, and services.  

Sec. 3  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki has a rich history and has played a significant role in the development of Lagos. It was once a major port and is known for its strategic location on the coast. The city has also been a center for political and social activities, contributing to the cultural and economic landscape of Nigeria.  

Sec. 4  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki is home to several important landmarks, including the Lekki International Airport, the Lekki Development Authority, and the Lekki International Trade Centre. These facilities support the city's role as a key economic and commercial hub in Nigeria.  

Sec. 5  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki  
Lekki has a growing population and is experiencing rapid urbanization. The city is also known for its multiculturalism and is home to various ethnic groups. This diversity contributes to the city's vibrant social and cultural environment.  

Sec. 6  
Ẹ wo bawọn ṣọja ṣe doju ibọn kọ awọn ọdọ ni Lekki
